input.content:
# Fix Off-by-One Bug in `processItems`

## Context

Users reported that the last item in the array passed to `processItems` is never processed. The cause is a classic off-by-one error in the loop condition: the loop iterates `i < items.length - 1` instead of `i < items.length`, which causes the loop to stop one index too early, skipping the final element every time.

## Critical File

- **`src/utils.ts`** — contains the buggy `processItems` function

## The Bug (line 7)

```typescript
// BUGGY
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```

For a 3-element array `["hello", "world", "foo"]`, the loop runs while `i < 2`, processing only indices 0 and 1 — `"foo"` at index 2 is never reached.

## Fix

Change the loop condition to `items.length` (remove the `- 1`):

```typescript
// FIXED
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

This is the only change required. No other logic needs to be altered.

## Verification

Run the existing Vitest suite:

```bash
npm test
```

All tests in `tests/utils.test.ts` should pass, including:
- `"should process all items in the array"` — was failing (skipped last item)
- `"should handle a single item"` — was failing (skipped only item)
- `"should handle two items"` — was failing (skipped second item)
- `"should return empty array for empty input"` — was already passing
- `sum` tests — unaffected, already passing

content.text:
## Plan Summary

**Root cause:** Classic off-by-one error in `src/utils.ts`, line 7.

### The bug
```typescript
// loops while i < items.length - 1 → skips the last element
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```

### The fix — one character change
```typescript
// loops while i < items.length → processes every element
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

### Why it breaks
For an array like `["hello", "world", "foo"]` (length = 3), the condition `i < 3 - 1` evaluates to `i < 2`, so the loop only visits indices 0 and 1 — `"foo"` is never processed.

### Tests
The existing Vitest suite in `tests/utils.test.ts` already has tests covering this (e.g., `"should process all items in the array"`, `"should handle a single item"`, `"should handle two items"`). All of those are currently failing and will pass after the fix. Verified by running `npm test`.
